добавить интерактивное использование навыков персонажа и расширить возможности администраторов при редактировании чужих персонажей.\n\n1. Бросок навыка через лист персонажа\nТекущая проблема\n\nСейчас навыки персонажа отображаются только как значения, но отсутствует возможность быстро выполнить бросок прямо из листа персонажа.\n\nНеобходимо\n\nПри нажатии на навык должен автоматически выполняться бросок соответствующей характеристики.\n\nПример:\n\nИгрок нажимает:\n\nАтлетика +5\n\nСистема выполняет:\n\n1d20 + модификатор навыка\n\nи отправляет результат в чат бросков.\n\nТребования\n\nПри броске необходимо учитывать:\n\nбазовый модификатор характеристики;\nбонус мастерства, если есть владение навыком;\nдвойной бонус мастерства, если есть компетентность.\n\nФормула должна соответствовать текущей системе расчёта навыков.\n\nОтправка результата\n\nРезультат броска должен автоматически отправляться в существующий чат бросков.\n\nВ сообщении желательно отображать:\n\nимя персонажа;\nназвание навыка;\nформулу броска;\nвыпавшее значение;\nитоговый результат.\n\n\n2. Изменение даты создания персонажа администраторами\nТекущая проблема\n\nДата создания персонажа используется как основа для расчёта свободных дней.\n\nОднако при ошибочном создании персонажа или переносе персонажа между системами изменить её невозможно.\n\nНеобходимо\n\nПользователям с ролью:\n\nАдминистратор;\nвыше;\n\nдобавить возможность изменять дату создания чужого персонажа.\n\nОграничения\n\nОбычный игрок:\n\nне может изменять дату создания своего персонажа;\nне может изменять дату создания чужих персонажей.\nПосле изменения даты\n\nСистема должна автоматически:\n\nсохранить новую дату создания;\nпересчитать количество свободных дней персонажа согласно новой дате.\nОжидаемый результат\nИгрок может делать броски навыков прямо из листа персонажа.\nВсе броски автоматически отправляются в чат бросков.\nАдминистраторы могут корректировать дату создания персонажей для корректного расчёта свободных дней.\nДоступ к изменению даты ограничен ролями с соответствующими правами.","closed_by":null,"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/RattusRex/Kral/issues/113/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/RattusRex/Kral/issues/113/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null,"pinned_comment":null}

DAMAGE_PATTERN.match(damage.strip())\n    if not match:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"Неверный формат урона\")\n\n    count = int(match.group(\"count\"))\n    sides = int(match.group(\"sides\"))\n    if count < 1 or count > MAX_DICE_COUNT:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=f\"Dice count must be between 1 and {MAX_DICE_COUNT}\"\n        )\n    if sides < 1 or sides > MAX_DICE_SIDES:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=f\"Dice sides must be between 1 and {MAX_DICE_SIDES}\"\n        )\n\n    mod_str = match.group(\"mod\") or \"+0\"\n    modifier = int(mod_str)\n    return count, sides, mod_str, modifier\n\n\n@router.get(\n    \"/characters/{character_id}/attacks\",\n    response_model=list[CharacterAttackResponse]\n)\ndef list_attacks(\n    character_id: int,\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)\n):\n    character = get_character_for_current_user(character_id, current_user, db)\n    return db.query(CharacterAttack).filter(\n        CharacterAttack.character_id == character.id\n    ).order_by(CharacterAttack.id.asc()).all()\n\n\n@router.post(\n    \"/characters/{character_id}/attacks\",\n    response_model=CharacterAttackResponse\n)\ndef create_attack(\n character = get_character_for_current_user(character_id, current_user, db)\n    attack = get_attack_for_character(character, attack_id, db)\n    attack_roll = random.randint(1, 20)\n    total = attack_roll + attack.attack_bonus\n    bonus_text = f\"+{attack.attack_bonus}\" if attack.attack_bonus >= 0 else str(attack.attack_bonus)\n    create_roll_chat_message(\n        db=db,\n        user=current_user,\n        formula=f\"1d20{bonus_text}\",\n        rolls=[attack_roll],\n        total=total,\n        content=(\n            f\"{current_user.username}: {character.name} атакует Итог: {total}.\"\n        )\n    )\n    db.commit()\n\n    return {\n        \"attack_id\": attack.id,\n        \"name\": attack.name,\n        \"roll\": attack_roll,\n        \"bonus\": attack.attack_bonus,\n        \"total\": total,\n        \"damage\": attack.damage\n    }\n\n\n@router.post(\n    \"/characters/{character_id}/attacks/{attack_id}/roll-damage\",\n    response_model=DamageRollResponse\n)\ndef roll_damage(\n    character_id: int,\n    attack_id: int,\n    db: Session = Depends(get_db),\n    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)\n):\n    character = get_character_for_current_user(character_id, current_user, db)\n    attack = get_attack_for_character(character, attack_id, db)\n\n    if not attack.damage:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"У атаки не задан урон\")\n\n    count, sides, mod_str, modifier = parse_damage_formula(attack.damage)\n    rolls = [random.randint(1, sides) for _ in range(count)]\n    total = sum(rolls) + modifier\n\n    formula = f\"{count}d{sides}{mod_str if modifier != 0 else ''}\"\n    mod_text = f\"{'+' if modifier >= 0 else ''}{modifier}\"\n    create_roll_chat_message(\n        db=db,\n        user=current_user,\n        formula=formula,\n        rolls=rolls,\n        total=total,\n        content=(\n            f\"{current_user.username}: {character.name} — урон {attack.name}. \"\n            f\"Кубики: {rolls}. Модификатор: {mod_text}. Итог: {total}.\"\n        )\n    )\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field\n\nfrom app.core.text_limits import MAX_CHAT_MESSAGE_LENGTH\n\n\nclass app.schemas.inventory import InventoryResponse\n\nMIN_CHARACTER_LEVEL = 1\nMAX_CHARACTER_LEVEL = 20\nSKILL_KEYS = {\n    \"acrobatics\", \"animal_handling\", \"arcana\", \"athletics\", \"deception\",\n    \"history\", \"insight\", \"intimidation\", \"investigation\", \"medicine\",\n    \"nature\", \"perception\", \"performance\", \"persuasion\", \"religion\",\n    \"sleight_of_hand\", \"stealth\", \"survival\",\n}\nABILITY_KEYS = {\n    \"strength\", \"dexterity\", \"constitution\",\n    \"intelligence\", \"wisdom\", \"charisma\",\n}\n\n\nclass SkillSettings(BaseModel):\n    skill_proficiencies: Optional[list[str]] = None\n    skill_expertise: Optional[list[str]] = None\n    saving_throw_proficiencies: Optional[list[str]] = None\n\n    @model_validator(mode=\"after\")\n    def validate_skill_settings(self):\n        proficiencies = set(self.skill_proficiencies or [])\n        expertise = set(self.skill_expertise or [])\n        unknown = (proficiencies | expertise) - SKILL_KEYS\n        if unknown:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Неизвестные навыки: {', '.join(sorted(unknown))}\")\n        if not expertise <= proficiencies:\n            raise ValueError(\"Компетентность требует владения навыком\")\n        if self.skill_proficiencies is not None:\n            self.skill_proficiencies = sorted(proficiencies)\n        if self.skill_expertise is not None:\n            self.skill_expertise = sorted(expertise)\n        saving_throws = set(self.saving_throw_proficiencies or [])\n        unknown_saving_throws = saving_throws - ABILITY_KEYS\n        if unknown_saving_throws:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Неизвестные спасброски: {', '.join(sorted(unknown_saving_throws))}\"\n            )\n        if self.saving_throw_proficiencies is not None:\n            self.saving_throw_proficiencies = sorted(saving_throws)\n        return self\n\n\nclass CharacterCreate(SkillSettings):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"forbid\")\n\n    name: str\n    class_name: str\n    level: int = Field(ge=MIN_CHARACTER_LEVEL,    </div>\n            <form className=\"mt-4 grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-[1fr_120px_1fr_auto]\" onSubmit={createAttack}>\n              <input className=\"field\" placeholder=\"Название атаки\" onChange={(event) => setAttackForm({ ...attackForm, attack_bonus: Number(event.target.value) })} />\n              <input className=\"field\" placeholder=\"Урон, например 1d8+3 рубящий\" value={attackForm.damage} onChange={(event) => setAttackForm({ ...attackForm, damage: event.target.value })} />\n              <button className=\"btn\" disabled={!attackForm.name.trim()} type=\"submit\"><Plus size={16} />Добавить</button>\n            </form>\n            {attackError && <p className=\"mt-3 text-sm text-red-300\">{attackError}</p>}\n            <div className=\"mt-4 space-y-3\">\n              {attacks.map((attack) => (\n                <div className=\"flex flex-wrap items-center justify-between gap-3 rounded-md border border-white/10 px-3 py-3\" key={attack.id}>\n                  <div>\n                    <div className=\"font-semibold\">{attack.name}</div>\n                    <div className=\"text-sm text-white/60\">Попадание: {signed(attack.attack_bonus)} · Урон: {attack.damage || \"-\"}</div>\n                  </div>\n                  <div className=\"flex flex-wrap gap-2\">\n                    <button className=\"btn-secondary\" onClick={() => rollAttack(attack)}><Dice5 size={16} />Бросить атаку</button>\n                    {attack.damage && <button className=\"btn-secondary\" onClick={() => rollDamage(attack)}><Dice5 size={16} />Бросить урон</button>}\n                    <button className=\"btn-secondary\" onClick={() => removeAttack(attack)}><Trash2 size={16} />Удалить</button>\n                  </div>\n                </div>\n              ))}\n              {attacks.length === 0 && <p className=\"text-sm text-white/55\">Атаки пока не добавлены.</p>}\n            </div>\n          </section>\n        </div>\n\n        <InventoryPanel inventory={inventory} onChange={setInventory} characterId={id} transferTargets={transferTargets} />\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction AbilityCard({ label, short, value, onRoll }: { label: string; short: string; value: number; onRoll?: () => void }) {\n  const modifier = abilityModifier(value);\n  return (\n    <button\n      className=\"ability-card text-left w-full\"\n      onClick={onRoll}\n      title={onRoll ? `Бросить d20 + ${signed(modifier)}` : undefined}\n      type=\"button\"\n    >\n      <div>\n        <p className=\"text-xs uppercase text-white/45\">{short}</p>\n        <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-ember\">{label}</h3>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"text-right\">\n        <div className=\"text-3xl font-bold\">{value}</div>\n        <div className=\"text-lg font-semibold text-white/75\">{signed(modifier)}</div>\n      </div>\n    </button>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction SavingThrowCard({ label, short, value, proficient, proficiencyBonus: bonus, onRoll }: { label: string; short: string; value: number; proficient: boolean; proficiencyBonus: number; onRoll?: () => void }) {\n  const modifier = abilityModifier(value) + (proficient ? bonus : 0);\n  return (\n    <button\n      className=\"ability-card text-left w-full\"\n      onClick={onRoll}\n      title={onRoll ? `Спасбросок d20 + ${signed(modifier)}` : undefined}\n      type=\"button\"\n    >\n      <div>\n        <p className=\"text-xs uppercase text-white/45\">{short}</p>\n        <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-ember\">{label}</h3>\n        {proficient && <p className=\"text-xs text-emerald-200\">Владение спасброском</p>}\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"text-right\">\n        <div className=\"text-lg font-semibold text-white/75\">{signed(modifier)}</div>\n      </div>\n    </button>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction SkillsPanel({ character, onChange }: { character: Character; onChange: (character: Character) => void }) {\n  const proficiencies = character.skill_proficiencies ?? [];\n  const expertise = character.skill_expertise ?? [];\n  const bonus = proficiencyBonus(character.level);\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n\n  async function save(nextProficiencies: string[], nextExpertise: string[]) {\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const response = await api.patch<Character>(`/characters/${character.id}`, {\n        skill_proficiencies: nextProficiencies,\n        skill_expertise: nextExpertise\n      });\n      onChange(response.data);\n    } catch (saveError) {\n      setError(apiErrorDetail(saveError, \"Не удалось сохранить навыки\"));\n    }\n  }\n\n  return (\n    <section className=\"panel p-5\">\n      <div className=\"flex items-center justify-between gap-3\">\n        <h2 className=\"text-lg font-semibold text-ember\">Навыки</h2>\n        <span className=\"text-sm text-white/60\">Бонус мастерства {signed(bonus)}</span>\n      </div>\n      {error && <p className=\"mt-3 text-sm text-red-300\">{error}</p>}\n      <div className=\"mt-4 grid gap-2 lg:grid-cols-2\">\n        {skills.map((skill) => {\n          const proficient = proficiencies.includes(skill.key);\n          const expert = expertise.includes(skill.key);\n          const score = character[skill.ability];\n          const value = abilityModifier(score) + (expert ? bonus * 2 : proficient ? bonus : 0);\n          return (\n            <div className=\"grid grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto_auto_auto] items-center gap-3 rounded-md border border-white/10 px-3 py-2\" key={skill.key}>\n              <span>{skill.label}</span>\n              <strong className=\"text-ember\">{signed(value)}</strong>\n              <label className=\"flex items-center gap-1 text-xs\"><input type=\"checkbox\" checked={proficient} onChange={(event) => {\n                const nextProficiencies = event.target.checked ? [...proficiencies, skill.key] : proficiencies.filter((key) => key !== skill.key);\n                const nextExpertise = event.target.checked ? expertise : expertise.filter((key) => key !== skill.key);\n                save(nextProficiencies, nextExpertise);\n              }} />Владение</label>\n              <label className=\"flex items-center gap-1 text-xs\"><input type=\"checkbox\" checked={expert} disabled={!proficient} onChange={(event) => {\n                const nextExpertise = event.target.checked ? [...expertise, skill.key] : expertise.filter((key) => key !== skill.key);\n                save(proficiencies, nextExpertise);\n              }} />Компетентность</label>\n            </div>\n          );\n        })}\n      </div>\n    </section>\n  );\n}\n\nfunction CharacterFormPage({ edit = false }: { edit?: boolean }) {\n  const navigate = useNavigate();\n  const { id: idParam } = useParams();\n  const id = Number(idParam);\n  const [form, setForm] = useState(blankCharacter);\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n                  <td className=\"py-3 pr-3\">{character.route || \"-\"}</td>\n                  <td className=\"py-3 pr-3\"><Link className=\"btn-secondary\" to={`/admin/characters/${character.id}`}>Открыть</Link></td>\n                </tr>\n              ))}\n            </tbody>\n          </table>\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"mt-4 border-t border-white/10 pt-4\">\n          <PaginationControls\n            page={characterPage}\n            pageSize={characterPageSize}\n            pages={characterPages}\n            total={characterTotal}\n            onPageChange={setCharacterPage}\n            onPageSizeChange={(value) => { setCharacterPageSize(value); When the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download failed, so do not call Read on that file. When images are from GitHub issues or PRs, such as URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\", use: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token $(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\"\n   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/RattusRex/Kral/issues/113.\n   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner RattusRex [keywords].\n   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.\n   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.\n   - When the issue is not defined clearly enough, write a comment with clarifying questions.\n   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.\n   - When you are fixing a bug, find the actual root cause first and run as many experiments as needed.\n   - When you are fixing a bug and the code does not have enough tracing or logs, add them and keep them in the code with the default state switched off.\n   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has three different comment types with different API endpoints:\n      1. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/RattusRex/Kral/issues/116/comments --paginate\n      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/RattusRex/Kral/pulls/116/reviews --paginate\n      Note: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" only returns conversation comments and misses review comments.\n   - When you need the latest comments on the issue, use gh api repos/RattusRex/Kral/issues/113/comments --paginate.\n\nSolution development and testing.\n   - When issue is solvable, first create a test that reproduces the problem, then implement the fix.\n   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.\n   - When coding, commit each atomic step that is useful on its own to the pull request branch so interrupted work remains preserved in the pull request.\n   - When you test:\n      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;\n      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.\n   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.\n   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.\n   - When you write or modify tests, consider setting reasonable timeouts at test, suite, and CI job levels so failures surface quickly instead of hanging.\n   - When you see repeated test timeout patterns in CI, investigate the root cause rather than increasing timeouts.\n   - When the issue is unclear, write a comment on the issue with questions.\n   - When you encounter any problems that you are unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.\n   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 116 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.\n\nReproducible testing.\n   - When fixing a bug, create a test that reproduces the problem before implementing the fix. [2026-07-10T19:09:27.638Z] [INFO] {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_15","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Epoch of Catastrophe Assistant\n\nPrototype web application for D&D 2014 open-table bookkeeping: characters, inventory, currency, karma, shop searches, and GM administration.\n\n## Backend Setup\n\n1. Install Python 3.11+.\n2. Create and activate a virtual environment:\n   ```bash\n   python -m venv .venv\n   source .venv/bin/activate\n   ```\n3. Install dependencies:\n   ```bash\n   pip install -r requirements.txt\n   ```\n4. Create PostgreSQL database `EpohaTruda` or set a custom `DATABASE_URL`.\n   Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in all required values:\n   ```bash\n   cp .env.example .env\n   ```\n   The required variables are:\n   - `DATABASE_URL` — PostgreSQL connection string.\n   - `SECRET_KEY` — secret used to sign JWT tokens. Generate one with:\n     ```bash\n     python -c \"import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))\"\n     ```\n   - `ADMIN_PASSWORD` — password for the default `admin` owner account.\n   - `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` — comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins (e.g. `https://yourdomain.com`).\n5. Run FastAPI (development):\n   ```bash\n   uvicorn app.main:app --reload\n   ```\n\nSwagger documentation: http://localhost:8000/docs\n\nNotable protected API routes:\n\n- `GET /api/leaderboard` returns users ranked by karma.\n- `GET/POST /api/chat/messages` stores general chat messages and `/r` roll commands.\n- `POST /api/dice/roll` rolls formulas such as `/r 2d6` or `/r 1d37` and stores the result in the rolls channel.\n- `PATCH /api/characters/{id}/inventory/notes` saves free-form inventory notes.\n- `GET/POST /api/characters/{id}/attacks` manages attack rows, and `POST /api/characters/{id}/attacks/{attack_id}/roll` records attack rolls.\n- `GET /api/shop/magic-items` searches `magicvariants.json` and returns shop-eligible common, uncommon, and rare magic items.\n- `POST /api/admin/users/{id}/role` lets an **owner** assign any user role (`owner`, `head_admin`, `admin`, or `player`). A **head admin** may use the same endpoint to manage `admin` and `player` roles only.\n- `GET /api/characters/{id}/calendar` and `POST /api/characters/{id}/calendar/downtime` let players view their character calendar and **add** character busy days. `PATCH`/`DELETE /api/characters/{id}/calendar/downtime/{entry_id}` edit or remove entries and are **restricted to administrators** — a player request is rejected with `403`. Admins may manage the calendar of *any* character.\n- `POST /api/characters/{id}/calendar/work` reserves a work period, calculates earnings from the campaign tool-proficiency table, credits the character wallet, and records the operation in the finance log. Completed work entries are immutable because their earnings have already been paid.\n- Manual calendar entries cannot overlap another entry for the same character or unit calendar. Duplicate and intersecting spans return `409 Conflict`; adjacent spans and the same dates on separate actor calendars remain valid.\n- `GET /api/characters/{id}/calendar/agents/{personal_hireling|simulacrum}` shows a granted unit's own calendar. `POST`/`PATCH`/`DELETE /api/characters/{id}/calendar/agents/{agent}/downtime...` are admin-only manual busy-day controls for those units.\n- `GET /api/admin/calendar-logs` returns the audit trail of administrative calendar changes (who, which character, action type, timestamp), filterable by `character_id`, `user_id`, `action`, and date range.\n- `GET /api/admin/grant-logs` returns the administrator resource-grant audit trail (karma, XP, gold, and items), filterable by player, character, administrator, operation type, and date. Every grant request requires a non-empty `reason`.\n- `POST /api/karma-shop/xp`, `/purchases`, and `/resurrect` atomically spend the authenticated player's karma on XP, persistent goods/openers, or an eligible dead character's resurrection. `GET /api/karma-shop/purchases` returns the player's saved goods and openers.\n- `GET /api/admin/karma-shop-logs` returns the complete Karma Shop audit trail, including the user, optional character, purchase type, cost, timestamp, and character level captured for character purchases.\n- `GET /api/admin/characters` includes each character's assembled date and free-day totals, plus separate free-day totals for a personal hireling and simulacrum when those units are enabled.\n- `PATCH /api/admin/characters/{id}` lets administrators correct a character's appearance date; calendar totals are recalculated from that date. Only owners and head administrators may delete characters.\n\n## Calendar Permissions\n\nThe character calendar (busy/free-day tracking) keeps the game timeline\nimmutable for players so already-spent time cannot be rewritten:\n\n- **🎮 Player** — may view their character calendar, free days and busy-day\n  history, and **add** character busy days, but **cannot** delete or edit\n  existing entries. A player cannot grant, remove, or manually spend personal\n  hireling or simulacrum time.\n- **🛠 Admin / 🛡 Head Admin / 👑 Owner** — may add, edit and delete busy days\n  for **any** character, personal hireling, or simulacrum in order to correct\n  calendar mistakes.\n\nThese rules are enforced on the backend, so hiding the buttons in the UI is not\nthe only safeguard — a direct API call from a player is rejected. Every\nadministrative change (create, edit, delete) is written to a calendar audit log\nrecording who acted, on which character, the action type, and the timestamp.\n\n## Shop Search Actors\n\nMarket searches can be performed by four actors:\n\n| Actor | Spends days | Spends whose days | Gold cost |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| Character | yes | the character's own calendar | no |\n| Personal hireling | yes | the personal hireling's own calendar | no |\n| Simulacrum | yes | the simulacrum's own calendar | no |\n| Paid hireling | no | none | hireling daily rate |\n\nThe personal hireling and simulacrum each have their own appearance date,\n`Расследование` modifier, and busy-day journal. Admins can enable them, edit\nthese values, and manually add/edit/delete unit busy-day records from the admin\ncharacter page. Players cannot self-grant or remove these units. Paid hirelings\nare external services: they charge gold for the rolled search duration, but\nthey never consume character, personal-hireling, or simulacrum free days.\n\n## User Roles\n\nAccess is controlled by four roles, from most to least privileged:\n\n- **👑 Owner** — full, unrestricted control, including managing every user, assigning and revoking the head-admin role, plus everything a head admin and admin can do.\n- **🛡 Head Admin** (Главный Администратор) — a trusted deputy of the owner. Has every administrative power of the owner **except** managing the owner (cannot change, block, delete, or appoint owners) and cannot grant the `owner` or `head_admin` roles. Can grant and revoke the `admin` role and manage players.\n- **🛠 Admin** — game-master tools: add/remove karma, grant items and currency, view logs, and manage game data. Cannot manage roles.\n- **🎮 Player** — default role for new accounts: manage own characters, chat, roll dice, and use the inventory.\n\nThe hierarchy is:\n\n```text\nOwner\n└── Head Admin\n    └── Admin\n        └── Player\n```\n\nThe seeded `admin` account is an **owner**. New accounts are created as **players**. Owners and head admins can change another user's role from the admin panel, subject to the restrictions above. These restrictions are enforced on the backend, so a direct API call cannot bypass them.\n\nCharacter sheets support proficiency for each of the six saving throws. A proficient saving throw adds the level-based proficiency bonus automatically. The available classes also include **Егерь** with a `d8` hit die.\n\n## Authentication Abuse Controls\n\nThe public login and registration endpoints include backend throttles to reduce\nbrute-force and account-spam abuse:\n\n- passwords longer than 72 UTF-8 bytes are rejected before bcrypt hashing or\n  verification;\n- repeated failed login attempts are tracked by submitted username/email and by\n  client IP, then temporarily return `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After`;\n- registration attempts are tracked by client IP before database uniqueness\n  checks and bcrypt hashing run;\n- repeated failures and blocked attempts are written to the application log.\n\nThe default backend limits can be tuned with:\n\n```text\nAUTH_LOGIN_FAILURE_LIMIT=5\nAUTH_LOGIN_IP_FAILURE_LIMIT=25\nAUTH_LOGIN_WINDOW_SECONDS=900\nAUTH_LOGIN_LOCKOUT_SECONDS=300\nAUTH_REGISTRATION_IP_LIMIT=10\nAUTH_REGISTRATION_WINDOW_SECONDS=3600\n```\n\nDocker deployments also apply nginx `limit_req` throttling to `/api/login` and\n`/api/users` before requests reach FastAPI.\n\n## Persisted Text and Request Limits\n\nThe backend rejects request bodies larger than 1 MiB, including requests sent\ndirectly to FastAPI. Persisted user text is also bounded independently:\n\n- chat messages: 2,000 characters;\n- inventory notes: 10,000 characters.\n\nChat history is returned in pages of at most 200 messages. These application\nlimits do not depend on nginx and therefore also apply during local development.\n\n## Frontend Setup\n\n1. Install Node.js 20+.\n2. Install dependencies:\n   ```bash\n   npm install\n   ```\n3. Run the development stack:\n   ```bash\n   npm run dev\n   ```\n\n`npm run dev` starts FastAPI on `http://localhost:8000`, waits for it to be reachable, then starts Vite. It loads variables from a project-level `.env` file automatically, so defining `DATABASE_URL` there is enough. If `DATABASE_URL` is not set in the environment or in `.env`, FastAPI startup fails with a configuration error instead of using an implicit database credential.\n\nThe Vite dev server proxies `/api` requests to `http://127.0.0.1:8000`. To use a different backend origin, set `VITE_API_TARGET`.\n\nIf you want to run services separately:\n\n```bash\nnpm run dev:backend\nnpm run dev:frontend\n```\n\n## Production Deployment\n\n1. Build the frontend:\n   ```bash\n   npm run build\n   ```\n2. Fill in all variables in `.env` (see Backend Setup step 4 above).\n   In production `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` must list only your actual domain(s).\n   The frontend build reads project-level `.env` values too:\n   - same-origin deployments can keep the default `/api` base and proxy `/api` to FastAPI;\n   - static builds served locally from `localhost` automatically call `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api`;\n   - static builds served from a different origin can set `VITE_API_TARGET=https://backend.example.com`;\n   - non-standard API paths can set `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://backend.example.com/api`.\n3. Start the backend without `--reload`:\n   ```bash\n   npm run start:backend\n   # or directly:\n   uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n   # or with gunicorn for multi-worker production:\n   gunicorn app.main:app -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker --bind 0.0.0.0:8000\n   ```\n4. Serve the built frontend from `dist/` with nginx (or another static server)\n   and proxy `/api` requests to the backend.\n\nFor a local production smoke test without a reverse proxy, set `ALLOWED_ORIGINS`\nto the static frontend origin, start the backend, build the frontend, and serve `dist/`:\n\n```bash\nALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:3000 npm run start:backend\nnpm run build\nnpx serve -s dist -l 3000\n```\n\n> **Security checklist before going live:**\n> - `SECRET_KEY` is a long random string (≥32 bytes), not `CHANGE_ME`.\n> - `ADMIN_PASSWORD` is a strong unique password, not `CHANGE_ME`.\n> - `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` lists only your production domain — no wildcards.\n> - `.env` is not committed to git (it is already in `.gitignore`).\n\n## Docker Deployment\n\nThe whole stack — PostgreSQL, the FastAPI backend, and the nginx‑served\nfrontend — can be run with Docker Compose. The browser talks to a single origin\n(nginx), which serves the built SPA and reverse‑proxies `/api` to the backend,\nso no CORS configuration or API‑URL build flags are needed.\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

[2026-07-10T19:15:00.270Z] [STDOUT] ## Summary

Fixes RattusRex/Kral#113.

- Added `POST /api/characters/{id}/roll-skill/{skill}` for all 18 D&D 2014 skills.
- Skill rolls calculate `1d20 + ability modifier + proficiency bonus` or double proficiency for expertise.
- Successful skill rolls are written to the existing rolls chat channel with character name, skill, formula, die roll, and total.
- Character sheet skill values are now clickable and show the latest roll result inline.
- The requested administrator-only character appearance date editing was already implemented on the branch baseline: `PATCH /api/admin/characters/{id}` accepts `game_created_at`, players cannot patch it, and free-day totals are recalculated from that date.

## Reproduction / Verification

Before this change, the sheet rendered skill modifiers but there was no skill-roll API endpoint and no click handler for skill rows.

Automated coverage added:

- `test_skill_roll_uses_ability_proficiency_expertise_and_logs`
- `test_skill_roll_rejects_unknown_skill_and_other_users_character`
- `character-skills.test.mjs` assertion that skill values call the roll endpoint

Local checks run:

- `.local-venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_api.py` — 100 passed
- `npm test` — 28 passed
- `npm run build` — passed
